articles.title: When AI Can Answer Everything, What Should We Still Learn?
articles.zh: 当 AI 能回答所有问题，我们还需要学习什么？
articles.deck: The value of learning is shifting from remembering answers to forming better questions, judgment and original connections.

sections.title: Answers are becoming cheap
sections.en: AI can explain a formula, summarize a book and suggest a plan in seconds. That does not make learning useless. It changes where the hard and valuable part begins.
sections.zh: 答案正在变得廉价，但这并不意味着学习失去价值，而是学习中真正困难和有价值的部分正在移动。

sections.title: Judgment is still earned
sections.en: A fluent answer can still be wrong, shallow or irrelevant. To judge it, I need enough knowledge to notice what is missing and understand consequences.
sections.zh: 流畅的答案仍然可能错误、肤浅或答非所问。判断力无法完全外包。

sections.title: Learning creates taste
sections.en: The more examples I study, the better I can recognize clarity, originality and craft. AI can generate options, but choosing what deserves to exist requires taste.
sections.zh: 学习也在形成品味：知道什么是清楚、原创和真正做得好的。

sections.title: My new rule
sections.en: I will use AI to move faster through repetition, but not to skip confusion too early. Confusion is often the doorway to a better question.
sections.zh: 让 AI 减少重复劳动，但不要过早跳过困惑，因为困惑往往通向更好的问题。

articles.title: I Failed My First Product—and Learned Who to Ask First
articles.zh: 我做砸了第一个产品，但学会了先问谁需要
articles.deck: I spent too long making something polished before discovering that the problem was mostly in my imagination.

sections.title: The exciting idea
sections.en: I began with a feature list and a name. That felt like progress, but I had not spoken to the people I hoped would use it.
sections.zh: 我从功能清单和名字开始，因为看得见的制作很像进度，但我还没有和真正可能使用它的人聊过。

sections.title: The uncomfortable test
sections.en: When I finally showed the prototype, people were polite. They understood it, but nobody had the problem often enough to change their habits.
sections.zh: 当我展示原型时，大家都很礼貌，却没有足够强烈的问题需要改变习惯。

sections.title: The lesson
sections.en: A prototype should test the riskiest assumption, not demonstrate how much work I have done. Next time I will test the need first.
sections.zh: 原型应该验证最危险的假设，而不是证明投入了多少时间。

articles.title: Why Some Stories Travel Further Than Others
articles.zh: 为什么有些故事传播得更远？
articles.deck: Shareable stories give people a useful feeling, idea or identity to pass forward—not merely something to watch.

sections.title: A story needs a carrier
sections.en: Content does not travel by itself. A person chooses to carry it into another conversation.
sections.zh: 内容不会自己传播，是人把它带进下一场对话。

sections.title: Compression without emptiness
sections.en: Strong digital stories remove everything that does not serve the central change. They are compressed, but not hollow.
sections.zh: 好的数字叙事会删除不服务于核心变化的部分。它被压缩，却不空洞。

sections.title: Give the audience a role
sections.en: The most memorable pieces leave a small gap for the viewer—to recognize themselves, disagree or tell someone else.
sections.zh: 最容易被记住的内容，会给观众留一个位置。

sections.title: A practical checklist
sections.en: Before publishing, I ask: Is there one clear change? Is there one detail worth remembering?
sections.zh: 发布前，我会检查：是否有清晰变化？是否有值得记住的细节？
